tests.name: returns default state before any pointer movement
tests.description: getState() returns centered default values (x: 0.5, y: 0.5, dx: 0, dy: 0, speed: 0, active: false) when no pointermove has been dispatched

tests.name: tracks normalized pointer position on pointermove
tests.description: Dispatching a pointermove event updates x and y to clientX/innerWidth and clientY/innerHeight respectively

tests.name: computes dx and dy from consecutive moves
tests.description: After two pointermove events, dx and dy reflect the normalized difference between the second and first positions
assertions: getState().dx equals (x2 - x1) where x values are normalized
getState().dy equals (y2 - y1) where y values are normalized

tests.name: computes speed as magnitude of delta vector
tests.description: speed equals Math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) after consecutive pointer moves

tests.name: handles touch input via pointerType touch
tests.description: Dispatching a PointerEvent with pointerType 'touch' updates position the same as mouse input
assertions: getState().x updates correctly for touch pointerType
getState().y updates correctly for touch pointerType

tests.name: normalizes coordinates to 0-1 range at viewport edges
tests.description: Pointer positions at (0,0) and (innerWidth,innerHeight) produce x/y values clamped to 0 and 1 respectively
assertions: getState().x equals 1 when clientX equals innerWidth
getState().y equals 1 when clientY equals innerHeight

tests.name: clamps coordinates that exceed viewport bounds
tests.description: Pointer positions beyond viewport edges (negative or > viewport size) are clamped to 0-1 range

tests.name: destroy removes event listeners
tests.description: After calling destroy(), dispatching pointermove does not update the state
assertions: getState() after destroy + pointermove equals state before destroy

tests.name: does not use localStorage or sessionStorage
tests.description: Initializing and using pointer tracking does not call getItem or setItem on Storage.prototype

tests.name: does not set cookies
tests.description: Initializing and using pointer tracking does not set document.cookie

tests.name: does not access indexedDB
tests.description: Initializing and using pointer tracking does not access window.indexedDB.open

tests.name: tracks active state on pointerenter and pointerleave
tests.description: Dispatching pointerenter sets active to true, pointerleave sets active to false

tests.name: dx and dy are zero on the first move
tests.description: The first pointermove has no previous position to diff against, so dx and dy should be 0

tests.name: getState returns a snapshot not a live reference
tests.description: The object returned by getState() should not change when new pointer events occur
assertions: snapshot captured before new event is not mutated after new event

Pointer position is tracked: tracks normalized pointer position on pointermove
normalizes coordinates to 0-1 range at viewport edges
Pointer velocity or delta is derived: computes dx and dy from consecutive moves
computes speed as magnitude of delta vector
dx and dy are zero on the first move
Mouse and touch both work: handles touch input via pointerType touch
No pointer history is persisted: does not use localStorage or sessionStorage
getState returns a snapshot not a live reference

setupNotes: Tests should create a target element, call initPointer(target), and dispatch PointerEvent instances on the target. Use vi.spyOn for privacy checks following the pattern in scaffold.privacy.test.ts. Set window.innerWidth/innerHeight via Object.defineProperty before tests.
